title: Understand the Subtle Design!
translatorNote: This translation is intended to carry Kalaignar's voice into clear, contemporary English rather than recast the letter as literary or academic prose. It preserves the source's argument, political directness, rhetorical questions, repetition, irony, factual detail, and paragraph order. Names, dates, figures, quotations, and intentional English expressions are retained. Where Tamil idiom cannot be reproduced literally without sounding unnatural, the English follows its sense and rhetorical force without adding claims absent from the source. The original Tamil is reproduced in full below the translation and remains the authoritative text. The Tamil section is a source-checked reading copy derived from the OCR and does not alter the canonical source file. Visual inspection restored the opening `உடன்பிறப்பே,`, which OCR omitted, and confirmed the terminal date as `27-6-2014`. One unambiguous Tamil glyph error and broken paragraph joins are repaired without changing the argument or order. `Udanpirappē` is retained in Tamil transliteration rather than flattened into “brother,” “sister,” or “comrade.” Literally evoking “one born alongside me,” Kalaignar uses it as a distinctive address of shared identity, equality, affection, and solidarity within the movement.
paragraphs: I know very well that when circumstances prevented the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam from winning even a single seat in the recently concluded parliamentary election, a few so-called friends among us rejoiced more than our enemies did.

They dreamed that the DMK could be finished off with this election. As we prepare ourselves for the struggles ahead, I offer this explanation so that my Udanpirappugal may understand what is happening.

They continue relentlessly to criticise the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam as though its history ended with this parliamentary election, and as though the movement could never rise again or walk forward. In particular, while attacking me in speech and writing, these critics disparage and malign our brothers who are as dear as life itself and the commanders whose labour built this movement. They raise suspicions about them in the public mind and dream that such propaganda will separate them from DMK cadres.

I have one thing to tell all of them: please look back at the history of the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam! This country understands, and we too understand, how every net cast by countless troublemakers and deceivers seeking its downfall has been torn apart and reduced to dust.

Our enemies are employing a stratagem. Because they lack the strength to attack the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam directly, their first step is to praise me extravagantly in speech and writing while insulting, maligning, mocking and discouraging commanders under my leadership, such as Stalin. They imagine that if they can then bring those men down too, the movement will be uprooted entirely.

That is why, in a single article, they praise and celebrate me and sing that I alone can protect this movement. In the very next line of the same article, they mock and ridicule those such as Stalin who may rise to lead it. They assume that praise of me will give some people a certain happiness. Before that happiness fades, they attack Stalin and others, ridicule them, and write articles portraying them as unfit even to run the party.

Do you know their calculation? They appear to believe: “Together we have already brought the Leader down somewhat from where he stood. This is the right moment to bring down the hardworking commander and others next.”

It is in that belief that a few people have pursued this tactic over the past several weeks. Seeing that our hearts have been cooled, they dream: “Were we not the ones who cooled them? Through the work we are now doing, can we not destroy this Dravidian movement so completely that not a blade of grass or root remains?”
They dream on, weave stories around their dream, create turmoil within the movement, and hope thereby to fulfil their desires and level the Dravidian movement to the ground.

Udanpirappē, let us give no room to those who nurture such fantasies. Whoever approaches us with deceit and flattering words, understand that they are always people who despise us. Keep away from them. Let us nurture our movement, which is as dear as life itself. I call upon you: come, come, let us pledge to hold the movement’s flag high!
If you understand the subtle design behind this leaflet, you can easily understand what I mean and what we must do.
